{"Question": "How do I initialize a cohort analysis pipeline with my pandas DataFrame?", "Context": "I have a pandas DataFrame with user activity data and I want to perform cohort analysis on it.", "Code_Example": "import pandas as pd
from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe

# Load your data
user_data = pd.read_csv('user_activity.csv')

# Initialize the cohort pipeline with your data
cohort_pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_data)

# Now the pipeline is ready for cohort analysis
print("Pipeline initialized with data shape:", cohort_pipeline.data.shape)", "Usage": "CohortPipe.from_dataframe(df)"}

metadata.description: Initialize a cohort pipeline with a DataFrame

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I create time-based cohorts using the registration date of users?", "Context": "I have user data with a registration_date column and I want to group users into monthly cohorts based on when they signed up.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts

# Create pipeline with your data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_df)

# Form monthly cohorts based on registration date
monthly_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='registration_date',
    cohort_column='signup_cohort',
    time_period='M',  # 'M' for monthly
    format_cohorts=True  # Format as "Jan 2023" instead of timestamps
)

# Now your dataframe has a new column 'signup_cohort' with values like "Jan 2023"
print(monthly_cohorts.data['signup_cohort'].unique())", "Usage": "form_time_cohorts(date_column='registration_date', cohort_column='signup_cohort', time_period='M', format_cohorts=True)"}

{"Question": "How can I create weekly cohorts instead of monthly ones?", "Context": "I want to do a more granular analysis by forming weekly cohorts instead of monthly ones.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts

# Create weekly cohorts
weekly_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='registration_date',
    cohort_column='weekly_cohort',
    time_period='W',  # 'W' for weekly
    format_cohorts=True  # Format as "Week of DD MMM YYYY"
)

# The dataframe now has a column 'weekly_cohort' with values like "Week of 01 Jan 2023"
print(weekly_cohorts.data['weekly_cohort'].unique())", "Usage": "form_time_cohorts(date_column='registration_date', cohort_column='weekly_cohort', time_period='W', format_cohorts=True)"}

{"Question": "How do I group users into cohorts based on their spending amount?", "Context": "I want to segment my users based on their spending behavior, such as high-spenders, medium-spenders, and low-spenders.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_behavioral_cohorts

# Define spending thresholds
spend_conditions = {
    'High-Spender': ('>=', 1000),
    'Medium-Spender': ('between', 250, 999),
    'Low-Spender': ('<=', 249)
}

# Create behavior-based cohorts
spending_cohorts = pipeline | form_behavioral_cohorts(
    behavior_column='total_spend',
    cohort_column='spending_tier',
    conditions=spend_conditions
)

# Check the distribution of users across spending tiers
print(spending_cohorts.data['spending_tier'].value_counts())", "Usage": "form_behavioral_cohorts(behavior_column='total_spend', cohort_column='spending_tier', conditions={'High-Spender': ('>=', 1000), 'Medium-Spender': ('between', 250, 999), 'Low-Spender': ('<=', 249)})"}

metadata.description: Create behavioral cohorts based on spending amounts
Create custom behavioral cohorts with complex conditions

{"Question": "How do I create cohorts based on user acquisition source and date?", "Context": "I want to analyze user behavior based on both when they signed up and their acquisition channel (organic, paid, referral, etc.).", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_acquisition_cohorts

# Create acquisition cohorts
acquisition_cohorts = pipeline | form_acquisition_cohorts(
    acquisition_date_column='signup_date',
    acquisition_source_column='utm_source',
    cohort_column='acquisition_cohort',
    time_period='M',
    combine_source=True  # Combines source and time period (e.g., "organic - Jan 2023")
)

# View the cohorts
print(acquisition_cohorts.data['acquisition_cohort'].unique())", "Usage": "form_acquisition_cohorts(acquisition_date_column='signup_date', acquisition_source_column='utm_source', cohort_column='acquisition_cohort', time_period='M', combine_source=True)"}

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate user retention rates for my cohorts?", "Context": "I want to see how well each monthly cohort retains users over time (classic retention).", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_retention

# First form the cohorts
pipeline_with_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='signup_date',
    cohort_column='cohort',
    time_period='M'
)

# Calculate retention
retention_analysis = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_retention(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='activity_date',  # Date of user activity
    user_id_column='user_id',
    time_period='M',
    max_periods=6,
    retention_type='classic'  # Classic retention (users who return in a specific period)
)

# Print the retention matrix
print(retention_analysis.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention'])", "Usage": "calculate_retention(cohort_column='cohort', date_column='activity_date', user_id_column='user_id', time_period='M', max_periods=6, retention_type='classic')"}

metadata.description: Calculate classic retention rates for cohorts

page_content:
{"Question": "What's the difference between classic, rolling, and unbounded retention?", "Context": "I'm not sure which retention type to use for my analysis.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_retention

# First form the cohorts
pipeline_with_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='signup_date',
    cohort_column='cohort',
    time_period='M'
)

# Calculate classic retention (users who return in a specific period)
classic_retention = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_retention(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='activity_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    retention_type='classic'
)

# Calculate rolling retention (users who return in this period or any later period)
rolling_retention = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_retention(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='activity_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    retention_type='rolling'
)

# Calculate unbounded retention (users who were active at least N times)
unbounded_retention = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_retention(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='activity_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    retention_type='unbounded'
)

# Compare the retention matrices
print("Classic Retention (Period 3):")
print(classic_retention.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention']['Period 3'])

print("\nRolling Retention (Period 3):")
print(rolling_retention.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention']['Period 3'])

print("\nUnbounded Retention (Period 3):")
print(unbounded_retention.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention']['Period 3'])", "Usage": "calculate_retention(cohort_column='cohort', date_column='activity_date', user_id_column='user_id', retention_type='rolling')"}

metadata.description: Compare different retention types: classic, rolling, and unbounded

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I analyze user progression through a purchase funnel?", "Context": "I have event data showing user actions (view_product, add_to_cart, checkout, purchase) and want to see conversion rates through this funnel.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_conversion

# First form the cohorts
pipeline_with_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='signup_date',
    cohort_column='cohort',
    time_period='M'
)

# Define the funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'checkout', 'purchase']
step_names = ['Product View', 'Add to Cart', 'Checkout', 'Purchase']

# Calculate conversion through the funnel
funnel_analysis = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_conversion(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    step_names=step_names,
    include_rates=True,
    cumulative=True  # Show cumulative conversion from first step
)

# Get the funnel data
funnel_data = funnel_analysis.conversion_funnels['time_cohorts_conversion']
print(funnel_data)", "Usage": "calculate_conversion(cohort_column='cohort', event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', funnel_steps=['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'checkout', 'purchase'], step_names=['Product View', 'Add to Cart', 'Checkout', 'Purchase'], include_rates=True, cumulative=True)"}

metadata.description: Analyze user progression through a purchase funnel

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I compare step-by-step vs. cumulative conversion rates?", "Context": "I want to understand both the step-by-step drop-off and the overall conversion from start to finish.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_conversion

# Define funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['signup', 'profile_complete', 'first_action', 'subscription']

# Calculate cumulative conversion (from first step)
cumulative_analysis = pipeline | calculate_conversion(
    cohort_column='user_segment',
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    cumulative=True
)

# Calculate step-by-step conversion
stepwise_analysis = pipeline | calculate_conversion(
    cohort_column='user_segment',
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    cumulative=False
)

# Compare the results
print("Cumulative conversion from signup to subscription:")
print(cumulative_analysis.conversion_funnels['behavioral_cohorts_conversion'][['cohort', 'subscription_rate']])

print("\nStep-by-step conversion rates:")
print(stepwise_analysis.conversion_funnels['behavioral_cohorts_conversion'])", "Usage": "calculate_conversion(cohort_column='user_segment', event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', funnel_steps=['signup', 'profile_complete', 'first_action', 'subscription'], cumulative=False)"}

metadata.description: Compare step-by-step vs. cumulative conversion rates

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate the lifetime value (LTV) of each cohort?", "Context": "I want to track how much revenue each monthly cohort generates over time to forecast future revenue.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_lifetime_value

# First form the cohorts
pipeline_with_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='signup_date',
    cohort_column='cohort',
    time_period='M'
)

# Calculate lifetime value
ltv_analysis = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_lifetime_value(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='purchase_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    value_column='revenue',
    time_period='M',
    max_periods=12,
    cumulative=True  # Show cumulative LTV over time
)

# Access the LTV matrix
ltv_matrix = ltv_analysis.cohort_results['time_cohorts_ltv']['ltv_matrix']
print(ltv_matrix)

# Calculate average 6-month LTV for all cohorts
six_month_ltv = ltv_matrix['Period 6'].mean()
print(f"Average 6-month LTV: ${six_month_ltv:.2f}")", "Usage": "calculate_lifetime_value(cohort_column='cohort', date_column='purchase_date', user_id_column='user_id', value_column='revenue', time_period='M', max_periods=12, cumulative=True)"}

{"Question": "How can I compare non-cumulative vs. cumulative LTV analysis?", "Context": "I want to see both the revenue generated in each period and the cumulative revenue over time.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_lifetime_value

# First form the cohorts
pipeline_with_cohorts = pipeline | form_time_cohorts(
    date_column='signup_date',
    cohort_column='cohort',
    time_period='M'
)

# Calculate cumulative LTV
cumulative_ltv = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_lifetime_value(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='purchase_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    value_column='revenue',
    cumulative=True
)

# Calculate non-cumulative LTV (revenue per period)
period_ltv = pipeline_with_cohorts | calculate_lifetime_value(
    cohort_column='cohort',
    date_column='purchase_date',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    value_column='revenue',
    cumulative=False
)

# Compare the results
print("Cumulative LTV over time:")
print(cumulative_ltv.cohort_results['time_cohorts_ltv']['ltv_matrix'].iloc[0])

print("\nPeriod-by-period revenue:")
print(period_ltv.cohort_results['time_cohorts_ltv']['ltv_matrix'].iloc[0])", "Usage": "calculate_lifetime_value(cohort_column='cohort', date_column='purchase_date', user_id_column='user_id', value_column='revenue', cumulative=False)"}

metadata.description: Compare non-cumulative vs. cumulative LTV analysis

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I chain multiple cohort analyses together?", "Context": "I want to form cohorts and then run both retention and LTV analyses on them.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import (
    CohortPipe, 
    form_time_cohorts, 
    calculate_retention, 
    calculate_lifetime_value
)

# Start with the pipeline and form cohorts
analysis_pipeline = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_data)
    | form_time_cohorts(
        date_column='signup_date',
        cohort_column='cohort',
        time_period='M'
    )
)

# Add retention analysis
retention_pipeline = (
    analysis_pipeline
    | calculate_retention(
        cohort_column='cohort',
        date_column='login_date',
        user_id_column='user_id',
        retention_type='classic'
    )
)

# Add LTV analysis
complete_pipeline = (
    retention_pipeline
    | calculate_lifetime_value(
        cohort_column='cohort',
        date_column='purchase_date',
        user_id_column='user_id',
        value_column='revenue'
    )
)

# Now we have both retention and LTV analyses
print("Retention Matrix:")
print(complete_pipeline.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention'])

print("\nLTV Matrix:")
print(complete_pipeline.cohort_results['time_cohorts_ltv']['ltv_matrix'])", "Usage": "CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_data) | form_time_cohorts(...) | calculate_retention(...) | calculate_lifetime_value(...)"}

{"Question": "How do I combine behavioral and time-based cohort analysis?", "Context": "I want to compare retention metrics between different user segments (high/medium/low value) and also see how these segments perform over time.", "Code_Example": "from cohortanalysistools import (
    CohortPipe, 
    form_behavioral_cohorts, 
    form_time_cohorts, 
    calculate_retention
)

# First create behavioral cohorts
value_segments = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_data)
    | form_behavioral_cohorts(
        behavior_column='lifetime_spend',
        cohort_column='value_segment',
        conditions={
            'High Value': ('>=', 500),
            'Medium Value': ('between', 100, 499),
            'Low Value': ('<', 100)
        }
    )
)

# Then add time cohorts (time of acquisition)
multi_cohort = (
    value_segments
    | form_time_cohorts(
        date_column='signup_date',
        cohort_column='time_cohort',
        time_period='Q'  # Quarterly
    )
)

# Calculate retention for value segments
value_retention = (
    value_segments
    | calculate_retention(
        cohort_column='value_segment',
        date_column='activity_date',
        user_id_column='user_id'
    )
)

# Calculate retention for time cohorts
time_retention = (
    multi_cohort
    | calculate_retention(
        cohort_column='time_cohort',
        date_column='activity_date',
        user_id_column='user_id'
    )
)

# Compare retention between high and low value users
value_matrix = value_retention.retention_matrices['behavioral_cohorts_retention']
print("Month 3 retention by value segment:")
print(value_matrix['Period 3'])

# Look at quarterly trends
time_matrix = time_retention.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention']
print("\nRetention trends by quarter:")
print(time_matrix['Period 1'])  # Month 1 retention across quarters", "Usage": "pipeline | form_behavioral_cohorts(behavior_column='lifetime_spend', cohort_column='value_segment', conditions={'High Value': ('>=', 500), 'Medium Value': ('between', 100, 499), 'Low Value': ('<', 100)})"}

metadata.description: Combine behavioral and time-based cohort analysis

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I create custom visualizations from the cohort analysis results?", "Context": "I want to create custom charts from the cohort analysis results for a presentation.", "Code_Example": "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from cohortanalysistools import CohortPipe, form_time_cohorts, calculate_retention

# Run the analysis
retention_results = (
    CohortPipe.from_dataframe(user_data)
    | form_time_cohorts(
        date_column='signup_date',
        cohort_column='cohort',
        time_period='M'
    )
    | calculate_retention(
        cohort_column='cohort',
        date_column='activity_date',
        user_id_column='user_id',
        max_periods=6
    )
)

# Get the retention matrix
retention_matrix = retention_results.retention_matrices['time_cohorts_retention']

# Create a heatmap visualization
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
sns.heatmap(
    retention_matrix, 
    annot=True, 
    fmt='.0%', 
    cmap='YlGnBu',
    vmin=0, 
    vmax=1
)
plt.title('Cohort Retention Analysis')
plt.ylabel('Cohort')
plt.xlabel('Months Since First Activity')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('retention_heatmap.png')
plt.close()

# Create a line plot of retention by cohort
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for cohort in retention_matrix.index[-6:]:  # Last 6 cohorts
    plt.plot(
        retention_matrix.columns, 
        retention_matrix.loc[cohort], 
        marker='o', 
        label=cohort
    )
plt.title('Retention by Cohort')
plt.xlabel('Months Since First Activity')
plt.ylabel('Retention Rate')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('retention_by_cohort.png')", "Usage": "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8)); sns.heatmap(retention_matrix, annot=True, fmt='.0%', cmap='YlGnBu', vmin=0, vmax=1); plt.title('Cohort Retention Analysis')"}

metadata.description: Create custom visualizations from cohort analysis results
